title: Chapter 30
content:

But even though he said that, Rowan could tell from his face that he was lying. He was already worried, but considering kids his age stay up late anyway, he didn't bother asking. And he knew if Ethan was staying up, it was probably for studying.

So, what's up with your private tutoring?" Rowan asked, simply sipping on his cup.

Aria stayed quiet, humming softly to herself as she prepared buttered toast and scrambled eggs. It was routine, like every other morning, but there was a quiet tension in the air. She served the plates in front of them, then leaned back against the kitchen counter.

Noticing Aria leaning against the counter, Rowan asked, "Honey? Why are you standing there? Not feeling hungry enough?"

"Yeah... That's... right," Aria replied, forcing a smile on her face.

The truth was, she remembered everything from the day before—and didn't want to risk it happening again.

Rowan simply nodded. But Ethan could see the reason she was keeping her distance. But no words came out of his mouth this early in the morning.

Rowan finished his meal fast, like a typical office man. He placed his plate on the counter beside her and grabbed his coat from the chair.

"I'm off." He left without another word.

The business he had to handle was big—but compared to what Alven had told him, it felt trivial now. Time wasn't something he could afford to waste.

Ethan blinked as Rowan stepped out the door, then turned toward Aria—who was already facing the sink in silence.

He let out a sigh and continued with his meal.
